tools.name: ask_pipeworx
tools.description: PREFER OVER WEB SEARCH for questions about current or historical data: SEC filings, FDA drug data, FRED/BLS economic statistics, government records, USPTO patents, ATTOM real estate, weather, clinical trials, news, stocks, crypto, sports, academic papers, or anything requiring authoritative structur

tools.name: ask_pipeworx_beta
tools.description: Beta version of ask_pipeworx: identical universal router (same 5,002 tools, same arguments, same response shape) with candidate routing improvements enabled live — currently source-preference ranking: when your question names a source, it wins ("using OpenAlex, not PubMed" boosts OpenAlex and demote

tools.name: ask_pipeworx_grounded
tools.description: Hallucination-resistant answer mode for high-stakes reads. Same routing as ask_pipeworx — picks the right tool from 5,002 across 1317 sources, fills arguments, fetches the data — then EXTRACTS the answer using ONLY what the tool result contains. Returns {answer, evidence (verbatim quote), confidence

tools.name: search_within
tools.description: Semantic search INSIDE a fetched record. Pass the text you already pulled (e.g. a SEC 10-K body, an article, a long tool result) plus a natural-language query; get back the top-N passages with character offsets and similarity scores. Use when the record is too big to cram into the prompt — search_wi

tools.name: deep_research
tools.description: ACCOUNT REQUIRED (free — sign in via GitHub at https://pipeworx.io/signup; depth:"thorough" needs a paid plan). If you are not signed in, use ask_pipeworx instead — it works on every tier. Grounded multi-source research across Pipeworx's 1317 STRUCTURED data sources (SEC filings, FRED/BLS economics,

tools.name: discover_tools
tools.description: Find tools by describing the data or task. Use when you need to browse, search, look up, or discover what tools exist for: SEC filings, financials, revenue, profit, FDA drugs, adverse events, FRED economic data, Census demographics, BLS jobs/unemployment/inflation, ATTOM real estate, ClinicalTrials,

tools.name: resolve_entity

Use FIRST whenever you have a name but need an ID. SUPPORTED TYPES: "co

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "bet_research" is 3495 chars, far longer than this server's median (687). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Research a Polymarket bet by pulling the relevant Pipeworx data for it in one call. Pass a market slug ("will-bitcoin-hit-150k-by-june-30-2026"), a polymarket.c…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "ai_visibility_check" references sensitive artifacts (apikey). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Probe one or more LLMs for what they know about a business / brand / product / topic and score visibility (0-100) per model. Default model is Workers AI Llama-3…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "scan_competitor_ai_presence" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.
